intent: Read colour rows from CSV and send each through one LLM that returns typed structured output fields — a blue amount (integer 0-100), a blue confidence (number 0-1), and a blue reason (one sentence). A downstream step must consume those typed fields to route each row, so the typed contract is genuinely used downstream and not just emitted.

options.prompt_template: Assess the perceptible amount of blue in the colour {{ color_name }} ({{ hex }}).

metadata.name: structured-llm
metadata.description: Typed multi-query LLM output consumed by a downstream gate.

semantic_expectations.topology: source -> assess_blue(llm typed queries) -> route_by_blue(gate on blue_amount) -> {strong_blue, weak_blue}(sinks)

runtime_assertions: The LLM node declares typed structured output fields (blue_amount integer, blue_confidence number, blue_reason string) via the typed multi-query authoring form.
The query name 'blue' prefixes each output suffix so the downstream row exposes blue_amount, blue_confidence, and blue_reason.
The downstream gate consumes the typed integer field blue_amount to route rows, proving the typed contract is used downstream and not merely emitted.
